rationale: The citing paper discusses Pocheptsova, Labroo, and Dhar (2010) in its theory section as key evidence that disfluency can have positive effects when interpreted through context-specific lay theories. It uses that finding to motivate its own core argument about the malleable meaning of processing difficulty. In the general discussion, the authors explicitly position their contribution as building on this prior stream by introducing a new service setting and a different mechanism centered on inferred agent competence.
relation_description: The citing study extends Pocheptsova et al. (2010) from special-occasion product evaluation to service-agent evaluation. It keeps the broader idea that disfluency can raise value in some contexts, but adds a new pathway in which disfluency signals task difficulty, which increases perceived agent competence and expected service value.
